title: P4 (QUA-1024) Fix P3 dispatch-key mismatch — restore 5x parallelism
description:
## Root cause

P3 wall-time is 25.8h for 130 runs (median inter-arrival 193s, no delta < 10s) when 5x parallelism would deliver ~82 min. Audit (Board Advisor 2026-05-09) traced this to a dispatch-key mismatch between outer reservation and inner re-resolve:

- `framework/scripts/p3_param_sweep.py:117-125` reserves with `phase="P3"`, `version="p3_sweep"`, `max_per_terminal=1`.
- `framework/scripts/run_smoke.ps1:125-133` (called inside the sweep) re-resolves with hardcoded `phase="P1"`, `version="smoke"`, `max_per_terminal=3`.

Result: two different dedup buckets in `D:/QM/Reports/pipeline/dispatch_state.json`. Terminal accounting incoherent. In practice the runs serialize on whichever terminal `resolve_target_terminal` returns first.

## Patch plan

1. `framework/scripts/run_smoke.ps1:125-133` — add two optional params `-DispatchPhase` and `-DispatchVersion`; preserve current defaults so existing P1 callers do not regress.
2. `framework/scripts/p3_param_sweep.py:88-104` — pass `-DispatchPhase P3 -DispatchVersion p3_sweep` when invoking `run_smoke.ps1`.
3. `.claude/commands/p3-launch.md:26` — remove the false claim 'P3 uses terminal=any — sequential, 1 terminal at a time. This is expected.' Replace with: 'P3 dispatches up to 5 parallel terminals; wall-time should be ~1/5 of the serial baseline. Verify via mtime spread in `D:/QM/reports/pipeline/<EA>/P3/QM5_*/`.'
4. **Bonus same diff:** `framework/scripts/p5_stress_driver.py:64,121-147` and `p6_multiseed_driver.py:110-126` have the same disease (blocking `subprocess.run` instead of `Popen` pool). Convert both to the pattern from `p2_matrix_launcher.py:130-139` (`DETACHED_PROCESS` Popen, round-robin over T1..T5).

## Acceptance

- P3 sweep on QM5_1003 closes in < 2h wall-time (baseline 25.8h). Verify via mtime spread in the P3 results dir — distinct timestamps for parallel terminals.
- `dispatch_state.json` shows the same `(phase, version)` bucket used at reservation and resolve.
- p5/p6 drivers show parallel terminal occupancy under sustained load (mtime spread test).

## Why this matters now

QUA-902 (P3 FAIL triage on QM5_1003) is the active critical path and is currently bottlenecked on this serial bug. Every P3 cycle costs ~25h instead of ~1h. Fixing this unblocks the V5 phase-3 cadence.

## References

- Parent: QUA-1024 (Board Advisor token-burn audit 2026-05-09)
- Audit data: `C:/QM/repo/.audit/agents.json`, `.audit/issues.json`
- Vorbild for parallel pattern: `framework/scripts/p2_matrix_launcher.py:130-139`
- DL-031 routing: V5 Framework Implementation project (framework/scripts/* + .claude/commands/*).
